steps.title: Use the left side of the plot to find the horizontal asymptote
steps.content: Graph all four exponential answer choices on top of the scatterplot. The correct curve should flatten near the left-side data, pass near the point around $(0,25)$, and then rise to the right.

steps.title: Use the $y$-intercept to narrow down further
steps.content:
At $x=0$ the scatterplot shows a point near $y \approx 25$. Each remaining choice's $y$-intercept is $a \cdot 1 + c = a+c$:
  • Choice B: $24+18 = 42$ — far too high.
  • Choice C: $1+23 = 24$ — close to 25.
  • Choice D: $6+18 = 24$ — close to 25.
Choice B is out.

steps.title: Compare growth at $x=3$ to break the tie
steps.content:
The plot shows points near $y \approx 42$–$46$ at $x=3$.
Choice C: $y = (1.55)^3 + 23 \approx 3.72 + 23 \approx 26.7$ — much too low.
Choice D: $y = 6(1.55)^3 + 18 \approx 6(3.72) + 18 \approx 40.3$ — matches the data.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
